content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of size NNN containing integers representing that the seats are located at (Ai,0)(A_i,0)(Ai​,0) coordinate. You have to allocate 111 seat each for ArupArupArup, AmanAmanAman and RahulRahulRahul such that the sum of time required for each of them to meet the other two (i.e. the sum of time required for ArupArupArup to meet AmanAmanAman, ArupArupArup to meet RahulRahulRahul, RahulRahulRahul to meet AmanAmanAman, RahulRahulRahul to meet ArupArupArup, AmanAmanAman to meet ArupArupArup, AmanAmanAman to meet RahulRahulRahul ) is maximum, given that you can walk at a speed of 111 unit distance per unit time in any direction. Print the sum of time required for each of them to meet the other two.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT , representing TTT test cases.

First line of each test case contains an integer NNN , representing the size of array AAA.

Second line of each test case contains NNN integers , representing array AAA.


Output Format

For each test case , print a single integer representing the sum of time required for each of them to meet the other two .
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases ≤ 10^6
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: the first line contains an integer N (the size of the array). The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1 … AN representing the x‑coordinates of seats (all y‑coordinates are 0).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single integer on its own line – the maximum possible sum of times required for each person to meet the other two (i.e., 4 × (maximum coordinate − minimum coordinate)).

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each test case the answer is 4 × (max − min). First case: max=5, min=-5 → 4·10=40. Second: max=4, min=-3 → 4·7=28. Third: max=1000000, min=-1000000 → 4·2000000=8000000.

solution.approach: For any three chosen seats a ≤ b ≤ c the unordered sum of pairwise distances equals (c‑a)+(c‑b)+(b‑a)=2·(c‑a). The required total (ordered) sum is twice that, i.e., 4·(c‑a). To maximise it we only need to maximise the distance between the farthest two selected seats, which is achieved by picking the global minimum and global maximum coordinates from the array. The third seat can be any remaining element. Hence answer = 4 × (max(A) − min(A)).
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case – a single pass to find min and max.
